The text describes a new type of backdoor attack on speaker recognition systems, called PaddingBack. The attack mechanism is as follows:

1. Adversaries create a backdoor dataset by poisoning a portion (ρ%) of the training samples. This is done by adding triggers to the samples and changing their labels to the adversary-specified label.
2. The poisoned and remaining clean samples are combined to create a backdoor dataset.
3. The adversary trains a deep neural network (DNN) on the backdoor dataset.
4. During the inference phase, the adversary can activate the backdoor by padding
